description: You use the elements in the smime folder to create digitally signed and/or encrypted MIME messages. You also use the services in this folder to process signed and encrypted MIME messages that are passed into the pipeline.

services.name: pub.smime:createCertsOnlyData
description: WmPublic. Generates a PKCS #7 certs-only S/MIME entity from an array of certificates.
This service can be used to develop mechanisms for transmitting certificates and certificate chains to other parties.

input_parameters.name: certificates
description: The certificates that are to be encapsulated within the S/MIME entity. Each byte[ ] represents a single certificate.

output_parameters.name: SMimeEnvStream
description: S/MIME entity.

usage_notes: For general information about MIME messages and using the MIME services, see the MIME-S/MIME Developer’s Guide .

services.name: pub.smime:createEncryptedData
description: WmPublic. Encrypts a MIME message.

input_parameters.name: envStream
description: MIME message that you want to encrypt (for example, the output produced by pub.mime:getEnvelopeStream).

input_parameters.name: recipientCerts
description: Note: When you have multiple recipients, createEncryptedData creates a single message that is encrypted for all recipients. It does not create a separate message for each recipient.

input_parameters.name: encryptionAlg
description: Note: If you have enabled the FIPS (Federal Information Processing Standards) mode , then Integration Server uses onlyTripleDES.

input_parameters.name: keyLength
description: This parameter is ignored if encryptionAlg is not RC2.

output_parameters.name: SMimeEnvStream
description: The encrypted MIME message.

usage_notes: For general information about MIME messages and using the MIME services, see the MIME-S/MIME Developer’s Guide .

services.name: pub.smime:createSignedAndEncryptedData
description: WmPublic. Deprecated - Replaced by pub.smime.keystore:createSignedAndEncryptedData. Digitally signs a MIME message and then encrypts it.

input_parameters.name: envStream
description: The MIME message that you want to sign and encrypt (for example, the output produced by pub.mime:getEnvelopeStream).

input_parameters.name: privKey
description: Private key of the party signing the message.

input_parameters.name: certificates
description: Optional. The certificate chain of the party signing the message, where each byte[ ] represents a single certificate in the chain. Certificates must appear in hierarchical order, starting with the signer's certificate in element 0. The following list shows how the elements of a complete chain would appear for a certificate that was issued through two intermediate CAs:

input_parameters.name: signerCert
description: Digital certificate of the party signing the message.

input_parameters.name: explicit
description: - true to generate an explicit (detached) signature. This is the default.
- false to generate an implicit signature.

input_parameters.name: recipientCerts
description: X.509 certificates of the recipients for whom this message will be encrypted. Each element in the list contains the certificate for a single recipient in the form of a byte array.

input_parameters.name: encryptionAlg
description: Note: If you have enabled the FIPS (Federal Information Processing Standards) mode , then Integration Server uses onlyTripleDES.

input_parameters.name: keyLength
description: This parameter is ignored if encryptionAlg is not RC2.

output_parameters.name: SMimeEnvStream
description: Signed and encrypted MIME message.

usage_notes: For general information about MIME messages and using the MIME services, see the MIME-S/MIME Developer’s Guide .

services.name: pub.smime:createSignedData
description: WmPublic. Deprecated - Replaced by pub.smime.keystore:createSignedData. Digitally signs a MIME message.

input_parameters.name: envStream
description: MIME message that you want to sign (for example, the output produced by pub.mime:getEnvelopeStream).

input_parameters.name: privKey
description: Private key of the party signing the message.

input_parameters.name: certificates
description: Optional. Certificate chain of the party that signed the message, where each byte[ ] represents a single certificate in the chain. Certificates must appear in hierarchical order, starting with the signer's certificate in element 0. The following shows how the elements of a complete chain would appear for a certificate that was issued through two intermediate CAs:

input_parameters.name: signerCert
description: Digital certificate of the party signing the message.

input_parameters.name: explicit
description: - true to generate an explicit (detached) signature. This is the default.
- false to generate an implicit signature.

output_parameters.name: SMimeEnvStream
description: The signed MIME message.

usage_notes: This service is superseded by pub.smime.keystore:createSignedData.
For general information about MIME messages and using the MIME services, see the MIME-S/MIME Developer’s Guide .

services.name: pub.smime:processCertsOnlyData
description: WmPublic. Extracts the certificates from a PKCS #7 certs-only S/MIME entity.

input_parameters.name: SMimeEnvStream
description: The certs-only S/MIME entity.

output_parameters.name: certificates
description: The extracted certificates. Each element in the list contains one of the extracted certificates represented as a byte[ ].

services.name: pub.smime:processEncryptedData
description: WmPublic. Deprecated - Replaced by pub.smime.keystore:processEncryptedData.
Decrypts an encrypted S/MIME message.

input_parameters.name: SMimeEnvStream
description: The encrypted S/MIME entity (for example, the output produced by pub.smime:createEncryptedData).

input_parameters.name: recipientCert
description: Digital certificate of the party receiving the message.

input_parameters.name: privKey
description: Private key of the party receiving the message (that is, the party whose public key was used to encrypt the message).

output_parameters.name: mimeData
description: MIME object containing the decrypted MIME message.

output_parameters.name: contentDigest
description: Message digest of the encrypted content, base64-encoded. (Some sites return this digest to the sender to acknowledge their receipt of the message.)

output_parameters.name: encrypted
description: - true indicates that the MIME entity is encrypted.
- false indicates that the MIME entity is not encrypted.

output_parameters.name: signed
description: - true indicates that the MIME entity is signed.
- false indicates that the MIME entity is not signed.

output_parameters.name: certsOnly
description: - true indicates that the MIME entity is a certs-only entity.
- false indicates that the MIME entity is not a certs-only entity.

output_parameters.name: stream
description: Conditional. The decrypted MIME entity.

usage_notes: This service is superseded by pub.smime.keystore:processEncryptedData.

If the decrypted message is signed or encrypted, mimeData will be empty, and the decrypted message will reside in stream. You can check the state of the signed and encrypted output variables to determine whether the decrypted message requires additional processing, and pass stream to the pub.smime:processSignedData or pub.smime:processEncryptedData service as necessary.

Important: You can examine the contents of mimeData during testing and debugging. However, because the internal structure of mimeData is subject to change without notice, do not explicitly set or map data to/from these elements in your service. To manipulate or access the contents of mimeData, use only the MIME services that Integration Server provides.

services.name: pub.smime:processSignedData
description: WmPublic. Verifies the signature from a signed S/MIME entity and extracts the message from it.

input_parameters.name: SMimeEnvStream
description: Signed MIME entity (for example, the output produced by pub.smime:createSignedData).

input_parameters.name: signerCertChain
description: Optional. Certificate chain of the party that signed the message, where each byte[ ] represents a single certificate in the chain. Certificates must appear in hierarchical order, starting with the signer's certificate in element 0. The following shows how the elements of a complete chain would appear for a certificate that was issued through two intermediate CAs:

output_parameters.name: mimeData
description: MIME object containing the extracted MIME entity.

output_parameters.name: contentDigest
description: Message digest (base64-encoded) that was recalculated by processSignedData.

output_parameters.name: signerCert
description: Signer's X.509 certificate.

output_parameters.name: encrypted
description: - true indicates that the MIME entity is encrypted.
- false indicates that the MIME entity is not encrypted.

output_parameters.name: signed
description: - true indicates that the MIME entity is signed.
- false indicates that the MIME entity is not signed.

output_parameters.name: certsOnly
description: - true indicates that the MIME entity is a certs-only entity.
- false indicates that the MIME entity is not a certs-only entity.

output_parameters.name: stream
description: Conditional. Extracted MIME entity.

output_parameters.name: verify
description: - true indicates that signature processing was successful.
- false indicates that signature processing failed. The signature could not be verified because an errorCode 1, 2, 3, or 4 occurred.

output_parameters.name: trusted
description: - true indicates that the signer certificate is trusted.
- false indicates that the signer certificate is not trusted.

output_parameters.name: errorCode
description: If no error occurred, errorCode will not be returned.

output_parameters.name: errorMessage
description: Conditional. Textual error message indicating what kind of error occurred while processing the signature. Error codes and messages are as follows:

children.name: errorCode
description: errorMessage

usage_notes: If verify is false, the errorCode and errorMessage values will indicate the error that caused the failure. Note that errorCode values 5 through 7 do not represent signature-validation failures and, therefore, do not cause the verify flag to be set to false.

If the extracted entity is signed or encrypted, mimeData will be empty, and the extracted entity will reside in stream. You can check the state of the signed and encrypted output variables to determine whether the extracted entity requires additional processing, and pass stream to the pub.smime:processEncryptedData service as necessary.

Important: You can examine the contents of mimeData during testing and debugging. However, because the internal structure of mimeData is subject to change without notice, do not explicitly set or map data to/from these elements in your service. To manipulate or access the contents of mimeData, use only the MIME services that Integration Server provides.

services.name: pub.smime.keystore:createSignedAndEncryptedData
description: WmPublic. Digitally signs and encrypts a MIME message.

input_parameters.name: envStream
description: The MIME message that you want to sign and encrypt (for example, the output produced by pub.mime:getEnvelopeStream).

input_parameters.name: keyStoreAlias
description: Alias of the keystore containing the signing key.

input_parameters.name: keyAlias
description: Alias of the private key to be used for signing.

input_parameters.name: explicit
description: - True to generate an explicit (detached) signature. This is the default.
- False to generate an implicit signature.

input_parameters.name: recipientCerts
description: A list of byte[ ] for the partner certificates for whom this message will be encrypted.

input_parameters.name: encryptionAlg
description: Optional. Code specifying the encryption algorithm to use. Must be TripleDES (default), DES, or RC2.

input_parameters.name: keyLength
description: This parameter is ignored if encryptionAlg is not RC2.

input_parameters.name: signingAlg
description: - SHA224
- SHA256
- SHA384
- SHA512

output_parameters.name: SMimeEnvStream
description: Signed and encrypted data as a stream.

usage_notes: You must use this service when you want to create a message that is both signed and encrypted.
For information about using aliases for keystores, truststores, and private keys, see Creating a Keystore and Truststore.

services.name: pub.smime.keystore:createSignedData
description: WmPublic. Creates signed S/MIME data.

input_parameters.name: envStream
description: MIME message that you want to sign (for example, the output produced by pub.mime:getEnvelopeStream).

input_parameters.name: keyStoreAlias
description: Alias of the keystore.

input_parameters.name: keyAlias
description: Alias of the private key of interest in the keystore.

input_parameters.name: explicit
description: - True to generate an explicit (detached) signature. This is the default.
- False to generate an implicit signature.

input_parameters.name: signingAlg
description: - SHA224
- SHA256
- SHA384
- SHA512

output_parameters.name: SMimeEnvStream
description: The signed MIME stream.

usage_notes: For information about using aliases for keystores, truststores, and private keys, see Creating a Keystore and Truststore.

services.name: pub.smime.keystore:processEncryptedData
description: WmPublic. Decrypts an encrypted S/MIME message.

input_parameters.name: SMimeEnvStream
description: The encrypted S/MIME stream.

input_parameters.name: keyStoreAlias
description: Alias of the keystore containing the decryption key.

input_parameters.name: keyAlias
description: Alias of the key used for decryption.

output_parameters.name: mimeData
description: The decrypted MIME message.

output_parameters.name: contentDigest
description: Message digest of the encrypted content, base64-encoded. (Some sites return this digest to the sender to acknowledge their receipt of the message.

output_parameters.name: encrypted
description: - True indicates that the MIME entity was encrypted.
- False indicates that the MIME entity was not encrypted.

output_parameters.name: signed
description: - True indicates that the MIME entity is signed.
- False indicates that the MIME entity is not signed.

output_parameters.name: certsOnly
description: - True indicates that the MIME entity is a certs-only entity.
- False indicates that the MIME entity is not a certs-only entity.

output_parameters.name: stream
description: The decrypted MIME entity.

usage_notes: For information about using aliases for keystores, truststores, and private keys, see Creating a Keystore and Truststore.
